title: Why USB-B never became as popular as USB-A or USB-C - Engadget
summary: By using two distinct connector types, the USB standard at the time made sure that people wouldn't accidentally connect two host or two peripheral devices together. USB-B features a squarish connector type and is broader than USB-A.
caption: output/2026-07-22/why-usb-b-never-became-as-popular-as-usb-a-or-usb-c-engadget.txt
